title: Gum on Denim
stainChemistry: Chewing gum is a hydrophobic polymer (polyisobutylene or chicle) mixed with softeners, sweeteners, and flavorings. On denim, the polymer adheres through mechanical bonding and surface tension. Warm gum is soft and sticky, spreading when touched. Cold gum becomes brittle and loses adhesion. The polymer is not water-soluble at any temperature.
whyThisWorks: Freezing makes the gum polymer brittle on denim, breaking its mechanical bond and allowing it to be scraped or cracked off in pieces. POG or citrus-based solvent dissolves any remaining residue by breaking down the polymer. Ice in a bag (not direct ice) prevents water damage while providing the cold needed. Picking at warm gum spreads it — always freeze first.
defaultAssumption: Assume stain age is unknown and possibly heat-set unless user specifies otherwise. Start conservative.

steps: 1. Freeze with CO2 spray or ice to harden gum completely
2. Crack and scrape off hardened gum with dull edge
3. Apply POG to dissolve remaining residue
4. Apply NSD to remove final traces. Flush with cool water
warnings: Always test on hidden area first

steps: 1. Place ice in a bag on the gum for 15 minutes until completely hard
2. Crack the frozen gum and peel/scrape off with a dull edge
3. Apply Dawn dish soap to remaining residue. Work gently
4. Rinse with warm water. Repeat if needed
diyProtocol.whenToStop: Stop if the stain hasn't improved after 3 attempts. Take to a professional for specialized treatment
warnings: Always test cleaning solution on a hidden area first
Air dry until stain is confirmed gone — heat sets most stains

neverDo: No chlorine bleach — removes indigo unevenly
No strong detergent on raw denim
fiberSensitivities: Indigo dye sits on surface — aggressive treatment causes uneven fading
Washed denim is more forgiving than raw

escalation.when: If gum stain persists after 2-3 professional treatment cycles, or if the stain has been heat-set, or if home treatment has not improved the stain after 3 attempts
escalation.whatToTell: Explain that the stain is gum on denim, how old it is, and what products were already used. This helps the professional choose the right chemistry without duplicating failed approaches

contextQuestions: Has it been exposed to heat (dryer, iron, hot water)?
Have you already tried treating it? If so, with what products?
deepSolveHooks.modifierNotes: Stain age and heat exposure are the two biggest factors. Fresh stains with no heat exposure have the highest success rate. Heat-set or aged stains may require professional treatment.
